reasoning: Stock prices over horizons of a few weeks are close to a random walk with a very slight upward drift. The yfinance tool combined estimate suggested 0.56 based heavily on an N=1 seasonal comparison, but the linear model estimate was only 0.45. There was a recent sharp jump in price from ~106 to ~123 in a short period, which increases the likelihood of a short-term mean reversion, balancing against standard drift and momentum. Furthermore, the second resolution date (Feb 17) occurs shortly after Mohawk Industries' Q4 2025 earnings call on Feb 12/13, which introduces a major volatility catalyst. Given the explicit instructions to stay close to 0.5 unless there is a very strong signal, I will predict 0.52 for the 1-week horizon (capturing a marginal upward bias unperturbed by earnings) and 0.51 for the 4-week horizon (where the earnings uncertainty pulls the probability closer to 0.5).
